type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: CO emissions decrease due to a decreased volume of landfill gas combusted in the flares.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Overall PM releases for 2015 were within 10% of the quantity reported in 2014.  Changes in PM emissions are due to the decreased volume of Landfill Gas combusted in the flare (decreased stack / point emissions), increase in total kilometers travelled by vehicles on unpaved roads (increased road dust emissions),  and increase in mass of material handled (increased material handling emissions).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Overall PM10 releases for 2015 were within 10% of the quantity reported in 2014.  Changes in PM10 emissions are due to the decreased volume of Landfill Gas combusted in the flare (decreased stack / point emissions), increase in total kilometers travelled by vehicles on unpaved roads (increased road dust emissions), and increase in mass of material handled (increased material handling emissions).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Overall PM2.5 releases for 2015 were within 10% of the quantity reported in 2014.  Changes in PM2.5 emissions are due to the decreased volume of Landfill Gas combusted in the flare (decreased stack / point emissions), increase in total kilometers travelled by vehicles on unpaved roads (increased road dust emissions),  and increase in mass of material handled (increased material handling emissions).

type.en: Reason why substance was added to the report
type.fr: Raison pour laquelle la substance a été ajoutée au rapport

comments.comment: Acrylonitrile is reportable in 2014, due to the change in reporting threshold.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: CO emissions increase due to 29% increase in LFG sent to the flares.  In 2014, three flares were in operation and their hours of operations increase substantially (50% increase) over last years hours of operation.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Significant changes in PM emissions are due to the increase in LFG combusted, decrease in weighted mass of vehicles travelling on unpaved roads, decrease in surface area of stockpiles (erosion) and increase in mass of material handled.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Significant changes in PM10 emissions are due to the increase in LFG combusted, decrease in weighted mass of vehicles travelling on unpaved roads, decrease in surface area of stockpiles (erosion) and increase in mass of material handled.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Significant changes in PM2.5 are due to the increase in LFG combusted, decrease in weighted mass of vehicles travelling on unpaved roads, decrease in surface area of stockpiles (erosion) and increase in mass of material handled.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The reported releases of CO decreased in 2012 relative to 2011.  This decrease was a result of a decrease in amount of landfill gas combusted at the facility in 2012.  The decrease in landfill gas combusted on-site can be attributed to the corrected amount of landfill gas sent to the off-site Cascade gas to energy facility.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The reported releases of PM increased in 2012 relative to 2011.  The reported PM releases increased as a result of increased number of vehicle-kilometers travelled on unpaved roads at the facility in 2012, which increases the particulate matter from re-entrained road dust.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The reported releases of PM10 increased in 2012 relative to 2011.  The reported PM10 releases increased as a result of increased number of vehicle-kilometers travelled on unpaved roads at the facility in 2012, which increases the particulate matter from re-entrained road dust.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The reported releases of PM2.5 decreased in 2012 relative to 2011.  This decrease was a result of a decrease in amount of landfill gas combusted at the facility in 2012.  The decrease in landfill gas combusted on-site can be attributed to the corrected amount of landfill gas sent to the off-site Cascade gas to energy facility.
